title: Duellum Veritatis pre-registered evidence task
description: Contract for a decisive numerical test, frozen BEFORE compute. Separates the evidence layer from the debate transcript: the metric, comparator ladder, thresholds, seeds and failure criteria are declared here, not improvised inside the run.

registered_before_compute.$comment: Must be true: the task is a pre-registration, not a post-hoc description.

registered_at.$comment: When the task spec was frozen. The gate requires this at or before compute_manifest.started_at when that field is present (ordering check); on its own it is a self-declared timestamp until external timestamping (Phase 5).

registered_by.$comment: Who registered the task (e.g. generator session id, OD).

pre_compute_event_id.$comment: Id of an event that existed BEFORE compute — the duel session id. The gate requires this to equal the sidecar source_session, binding the task to a pre-existing event the author did not mint after seeing results.

decisive.$comment: True if this task's result is treated as decisive for the verdict.

definition.$comment: Exact formula / construction of the statistic under test.

paper_faithful.$comment: True only if this implements the seed-paper observable as defined; a toy/proxy statistic is false and cannot back a scientific_result release.

evidence_basis.$comment: How the decisive observable is established. 'simulation' = finite-size numerical model only; 'semi_analytic' = analytic theory with numerical cross-check (e.g. DMFT verified by simulation); 'analytic' = closed-form/limit result; 'empirical_data' = tested on the system's real data. A paper-faithful observable measured by 'simulation' alone is a model result, not a paper-level finding: scientific_result requires a basis above bare simulation.

source_ref.$comment: Paper equation/section the observable reproduces, when paper_faithful.

model.$comment: Generative/data model under test (description and/or parameters).

comparators.$comment: Comparator ladder; at minimum a structured vs matched-random pair.

thresholds.$comment: Pre-registered decision thresholds (e.g. margin) WITH rationale. Living in the script instead of here is post-hoc.

variants.$comment: For detectability claims report raw AND folded; for directional claims declare signed/directional.

nulls.$comment: Null models / negative controls declared up front.

sensitivity_grid.$comment: Declared grid over C, d, T, behavioral covariance, structured/random comparators, seeds. Null = single setup (then scope is limited).
